title: Ram Patty Kulcha
description: Located in the busy lanes of Katra Ahluwalia near the Golden Temple, Ram Patty Kulcha Point is a legendary street food stall run by three brothers. It is famous for its unique triangle-shaped, flaky 'Patty Kulcha' filled with a spiced mixture of corn, peas, and potatoes, baked to crispy perfection in a traditional clay tandoor. Served with flavorful chole and their signature onion chutney.
address: Katra Ahluwalia, Near Jalebi Chowk, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Run by three brothers who maintain the secret recipe for their flaky crust
Instead of a standard flatbread, the patty kulcha resembles a flaky puff pastry baked in a tandoor
Arrive early in the morning as they regularly sell out before their official closing time
Extremely popular among local shoppers and pilgrims visiting the nearby Darbar Sahib
A rare non-traditional variation of the Amritsari Kulcha that has gained legendary status

title: Gian Chand Lassi (Gian di Lassi)
description: Gian Chand Lassi (popularly known as Gian di Lassi), located in Katra Sher Singh, is a legendary, decades-old institution celebrated as one of the oldest and most iconic lassi shops in Amritsar. It is famous for its thick, sweet, hand-churned lassi served in tall glasses, topped with a massive dollop of fresh cream (malai) and butter, with the 'Peda Lassi' being a highly recommended local favorite.
address: Katra Sher Singh, Near Regent Cinema, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Widely recognized as the pioneer of the 'Peda Lassi', where sweet peda sweets are blended directly into the yogurt
Every glass is prepared fresh using traditional wooden whisks (madhani) to retain thickness
Frequented by travelers and food writers seeking the most authentic Punjabi lassi experience
Located in the historic Katra Sher Singh area, making it a perfect afternoon refreshment stop
The consistency is so rich and thick that it is eaten with a spoon rather than drunk

title: Gurdas Ram Jalebi Wala
description: Located in a tiny shop just a few steps away from the Golden Temple, Gurdas Ram Jalebi Wala has been serving hot, piping, and crispy jalebis and gulab jamuns for decades. The jalebis are fried in pure desi ghee right in front of your eyes and soaked in sugar syrup, offering the ultimate sweet ending to your heritage tour.
address: Katras Ahluwalia, Near Golden Temple, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bulletPoints: Operates from a very small shop front, emphasizing quality and speed of preparation
Prepared using traditional cotton cloth squeeze bags to shape the loops in oil
Locals recommend pairing the sweet jalebis with savory rabri when available
Perfect quick snack stop during a walking tour of the Golden Temple corridors
Highly renowned for retaining the exact same crisp texture and syrup sweetness for generations

title: Beera Samosa Wala
description: Established in 1949, Beera Samosa Wala is a legendary street food joint located in Atta Mandi, near the Golden Temple. With over 75 years of history, it is famous for serving hot, crispy, and flaky samosas filled with spicy potatoes, served alongside their signature tangy aloo sabzi. The shop is also highly celebrated for its 'Satpura', a multi-layered crispy patty.
address: Atta Mandi, Katra Ahluwalia, Near Golden Temple, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bulletPoints: Founded right after partition in 1949, preserving traditional refugee snack recipes
Their unique 'Satpura' is a multi-layered patty that crumbles delightfully with every bite
Unlike typical chutney pairings, they serve their samosas drenched in a warm, spicy potato curry
An absolute must-visit for tourists exploring the old city lanes behind Darbar Sahib
Maintains an old-school, no-frills setup where snack batches are fried fresh in massive iron woks

title: Lubhaya Ram & Sons Aam Papad
description: Established in 1965 on Lawrence Road, Lubhaya Ram & Sons is a legendary establishment renowned for its authentic Aam Papad (mango leather). It is famous for serving a unique sweet and sour chaat made from layers of sun-dried mango pulp chopped and tossed with black salt, cumin, chili powder, and fresh lemon juice, alongside an assortment of traditional digestive candies (churan).
address: Lawrence Road, Opposite Arya Samaj Mandir, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Known for their distinct recipe where mango sheets are seasoned and shredded into a flavorful chaat
Offers a wide selection of mango varieties ranging from sweet (meetha) to extremely tangy (khatta)
A popular stop for locals to pick up post-meal digestive candies and traditional pickles
Located opposite the historic Arya Samaj Mandir, a central commercial hub in Amritsar
Perfect souvenir stop to buy packed sweet-and-sour mango sheets for friends and family

title: Kanha Sweets
description: Kanha Sweets on Lawrence Road is an iconic breakfast institution in Amritsar, widely celebrated for serving the city's finest Puri Chole. The meal features large, piping hot, crispy puris served alongside spicy masala chole and a unique sweet-and-tangy aloo ki sabzi, often paired with their famous rich Gur ka Halwa and sweet lassi.
address: Lawrence Road, White Avenue, Opposite Bijli Pehalwan Mandir, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Famous for their unique service style where potato and chickpea curries are refilled unlimitedly
The aloo sabzi has a signature sweet-and-sour (khatta-meetha) profile that pairs perfectly with spicy chole
Their Gur ka Halwa (jaggery semolina pudding) is widely regarded as a stellar sweet accompaniment
Arrive early on weekends to secure a table, as crowds gather for their traditional breakfast
Also features a popular sweet counter showcasing high-quality traditional Punjabi milk sweets
